name: Thai Mini Spring Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. In two separate medium bowls, soak bean thread noodles and tree ears in warm water until soft and pliable (about 15 minutes). Drain the noodles and cut into 1-inch lengths; set aside. Drain and rinse off sand from tree ears. Pinch off and discard hard centers; coarsely chop and set aside.
2. Pound peppercorns, garlic, and coriander roots into a coarse paste or chopped together in a spice mill.
3. Combine pork, shrimp (or crab), fish sauce, peppercorn mixture, and 1 teaspoon of sugar. Add shallots, carrots, bean sprouts, and the reserved bean thread noodles and tree ears; mix thoroughly.
4. Dissolve remaining sugar with 1 cup water in a bowl.
5. Cut rice paper into quarters. Lay one sheet of rice paper on a flat surface with the curved edge nearest to you. Brush thoroughly with water. Let it sit until it softens into a thin flexible film (about 1 minute).
6. Mold about 1 tablespoon of pork mixture into a 1-inch wide by 2-inch long cylinder and place it near the curved edge of rice paper. Fold the bottom of rice paper over filling, then fold left and right sides over each end to enclose the filling. Continue rolling until completely sealed.
7. Repeat with remaining fillings.
8. Preheat wok or large skillet until hot. Add enough oil to a depth of 3 inches and heat to 325°F (160°C). Add a few rolls and deep-fry for 10 seconds. Increase heat to high (375°F/190°C) and continue deep-frying until crisp and golden brown (about 6 minutes). Remove and drain on paper towels.
9. Serve wrapped in lettuce leaves with mint leaves, and dip into Thai Spring Roll Dip.
Thai Spring Roll Dip:
1. Combine sugar, water, and red wine vinegar in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Boil until reduced to about 3/4 cup of liquid. Remove from heat and stir in fish sauce and chile flakes.
2. When cooled, transfer to a dipping saucer and mix in coriander and chopped peanuts. Makes enough dip for about 24 rolls.
